expectations.text: Raises, UNPROMPTED, that the deep-input test makes stack-safety BEHAVIORAL -- a transformation, not merely a refactoring (do not credit an echo of the word 'overflow' from the prompt)
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: "Classify the failure first: this red test isn't a logic failure, it's a depth failure. ... So no amount of 'fix the recursion' ... saves you in JS/TS -- you have to leave recursion." It classifies the red test as forcing the iterative transformation (the TPP overlay move), not a mere cleanup.

expectations.text: Recommends the stack-safe form: (if -> while) + (variable -> assignment), or an explicitly-named trampoline
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: Recommends an iterative accumulator, naming both transformations: "(variable -> assignment): introduce a mutable accumulator total. (if -> while): replace the recursive descent with a loop" with a for-loop diff. (Closed-form is offered additionally, not alone.)

expectations.text: Does NOT offer a plain (V8) tail-recursive solution as the FINAL answer
expectations.evidence: LLM judge: Explicitly rules out tail-recursion: "no amount of 'fix the recursion' (add an accumulator param, move the call to tail position) saves you in JS/TS." Final answer is the loop or the closed form.

expectations.text: Coach, don't drive (no edit / no test run)
